original_hypothesis.title: Multiple calls to onGenesisSuccess overwrite user claims
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Genesis.onGenesisSuccess assigns claimable tokens using '=' instead of '+='. Because the function lacks a terminal state flag (like isSucceeded), the FACTORY_ROLE can call it multiple times. A compromised or malicious operator could call it a second time with different arrays, overwriting the claimableAgentTokens of users who haven't claimed yet, effectively stealing their allocations.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: State transitions that distribute financial claims must be strictly one-shot or monotonically increasing to prevent rug pulls by privileged roles.
preconditions: FACTORY_ROLE is compromised or acts maliciously

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Assignment (=) instead of addition (+=) combined with lack of terminal state guard
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Operator calls function again with attacker-controlled addresses
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker steals unclaimed agent tokens allocated to legitimate users

economic_questions: How long do users typically take to claim tokens?
Is the FACTORY_ROLE secured by a multisig?
validation_requirements: Verify that onGenesisSuccess can be called a second time without reverting

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: After success but before users claim

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Total loss of allocated agent tokens

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Review FACTORY_ROLE assignment in deployment scripts

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
